metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Ensure RebarBotNava is durably encoded as a Contributor: either add `CONTRIBUTOR_MEMBERS ?= RebarBotNava` as a documented default in setup-project.sh or a config file, or add a static `$GERRIT_SSH gerrit set-members Contributors --add RebarBotNava` call so the membership survives re-provisioning without relying on an externally-set env var.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'add RebarBotNava (member)' to Contributors, claiming it was 'applied live via REST as an admin.' No static evidence of RebarBotNava exists in any tracked file — the account is not referenced in setup-project.sh, project.config, CONTRIBUTING.md, or any other file in the repo. The CONTRIBUTOR_MEMBERS env-var mechanism is the intended convergence path, but the current scripts contain no hard-coded or default-value reference to 'RebarBotNava', making the membership assertion unverifiable from the codebase.
findings.location: Plan 'What' section ('add RebarBotNava (member)'); AC line 'RebarBotNava is in Contributors and NOT in Administrators'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Encode `RebarBotNava` (and any other intended Contributors members) declaratively — either as the default value of `CONTRIBUTOR_MEMBERS` in setup-project.sh or as a hardcoded `set-members --add RebarBotNava` call — so a fresh `bash infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh` produces the same Contributors membership as the live state.
findings.finding: The plan names 'RebarBotNava' as a Contributors member (AC2: 'RebarBotNava is in Contributors') and states it was added via REST, but 'RebarBotNava' appears nowhere in any tracked file — not as a default or hardcoded value in CONTRIBUTOR_MEMBERS, not in setup-project.sh, not in project.config, not in CONTRIBUTING.md. If setup-project.sh is re-run (e.g., for disaster recovery or a fresh environment), the Contributors group will be created with only Administrators — RebarBotNava will be silently absent, contradicting the stated policy.
findings.location: What section / AC2: 'RebarBotNava is in Contributors'; infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh lines 87–101
findings.suggested_fix:
In setup-project.sh, change the CONTRIBUTOR_MEMBERS default from empty to include RebarBotNava:
```bash
CONTRIBUTOR_MEMBERS="${CONTRIBUTOR_MEMBERS:-RebarBotNava}"
```
This preserves override-ability via the env var while making the default declarative.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Update `docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md` Section 6 to document the `Contributors` group, the `CONTRIBUTOR_MEMBERS` env var, and the exclusive Submit ACL — mirroring how `FEATURE_BRANCH_DRIVER_MEMBERS` and the `feature-branch-drivers` group are already documented there.
findings.finding: docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md Section 6 ('Step 3 — Gerrit project + LLM-Review label + submit requirement') is a direct consumer of infra/gerrit/project.config and infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh that documents the group provisioning model in detail, but it makes no mention of the new Contributors group or the Submit ACL. After this change, an operator following that doc to provision a fresh environment will not know to set CONTRIBUTOR_MEMBERS, will not know the Contributors group is required for Submit, and will not know that the Submit permission is now exclusive. This file is not enumerated as an edit target in the plan.
findings.location: What section lists edit targets; docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md lines 231–305

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command for AC3 that exercises the live submit path as a non-contributor (e.g. `ssh -p 29418 <gerrit> gerrit review --submit <change-id>,<patchset>` as a test Registered User, expecting a 'submit not permitted' error, OR a Gerrit REST call `POST /changes/{id}/submit` returning HTTP 403) and record the expected output.
findings.finding: AC3 ('A plain Registered User / Service User cannot submit; can still push refs/for/*') exercises the critical new live ACL path but provides no concrete proving command — no described test user, no Gerrit REST call, no SSH attempt to submit as a non-contributor — so the cutover to the new submit restriction has no end-to-end verification step documented.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC3: 'Verified by: attempting `POST /a/changes/{id}/submit` via the Gerrit REST API authenticated as a non-contributor Registered User and confirming HTTP 403 / "submit not permitted"; separately confirming `git push gerrit HEAD:refs/for/main` succeeds for the same user.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] For AC1 and AC2, add concrete re-runnable proving commands (e.g. `curl -s --digest -u admin GET .../a/projects/infra%2Fgerrit/access` to inspect the submit ACL, and `curl .../a/groups/Contributors/members` to confirm RebarBotNava present and Administrators group absent as a direct member).
findings.finding: ACs 1 and 2 claim states that are 'verified live' but supply no reproducible proving command, so a future executor or auditor cannot re-verify them (e.g. after a config rollback).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define and document a member-removal/offboarding procedure for the Contributors group (e.g., a `CONTRIBUTOR_MEMBERS` removal step in setup-project.sh, or an explicit admin runbook), and confirm setup-project.sh convergence is not additive-only.
findings.finding: The Contributors group has a defined CREATE path (via REST, admin-run) and an UPDATE path (setup-project.sh convergence for membership), but there is no defined RETIRE/offboarding path: if RebarBotNava or another contributor should be removed, no process or tooling is specified. Uncontrolled accumulation of stale members in a group that gates production landing is a security concern.
findings.location: ## What — 'Create the Contributors group … wire CONTRIBUTOR_MEMBERS convergence into setup-project.sh'
findings.suggested_fix: In setup-project.sh, make the CONTRIBUTOR_MEMBERS convergence set-reconcile (remove members not in the canonical list, not just add) and document in CONTRIBUTING.md or a runbook how to remove a contributor from the group.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that `setup-project.sh` sets Contributors group owner to Administrators (add `--owner-group Administrators` to the `gerrit create-group` call, or confirm via REST that ownership is correctly assigned after live creation).
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'Create the Contributors group (owner Administrators)' but setup-project.sh uses `gerrit create-group … --group Administrators`, which adds Administrators as a *member subgroup* — not as the group owner. The Gerrit SSH `create-group` command uses `--owner-group <group>` to set ownership; no such flag appears anywhere in setup-project.sh. If Administrators is not the owner of the Contributors group, non-admin group members could potentially be added/removed by whoever does own the group (defaulting to the creating account).
findings.location: infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh lines 92–94; Plan 'What' section ('Create the Contributors group (owner Administrators)')
findings.suggested_fix: Change the create-group call to: `$GERRIT_SSH gerrit create-group "$CONTRIB_GROUP" --description "…" --owner-group Administrators --group Administrators` — `--owner-group` sets the owning group; `--group` includes Administrators as a subgroup member (both are needed).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/back-out procedure (e.g., `git push origin HEAD:refs/meta/config` to revert to commit 4d5a28a, or restore the inherited Submit grant) in case the Contributors group is misconfigured post-deploy.
findings.finding: The plan restricts Submit access for all Registered Users (a behavioral breaking change already applied live) but provides no rollback/back-out path if the Contributors group is misconfigured or legitimate contributors are accidentally excluded.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Rollback' subsection under What (or a separate section): 'To revert: git push gerrit 4d5a28a:refs/meta/config, then remove the Contributors group if desired. This restores inherited Submit to all Registered Users while the group config is corrected.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief positive rationale in the plan (and/or a comment in setup-project.sh / CONTRIBUTING.md §2e) explaining what RebarBotNava's Submit use-case is — e.g., whether it auto-submits approved changes, and why that warrants Contributors membership rather than admin-on-behalf.
findings.finding: The plan does not state a positive rationale for why RebarBotNava needs Submit permission (i.e., membership in Contributors) rather than remaining a pure voting Service User. The 'Why' section explains restricting Submit to Contributors generally, but never establishes what RebarBotNava's concrete Submit use-case is — whether it auto-submits once both votes are MAX, performs supervised landing, or has some other workflow role. Without that rationale an implementer cannot verify the membership is correct or know when to revoke it.
findings.location: Plan 'What' section — 'Create the Contributors group … add RebarBotNava (member)'; AC line 'RebarBotNava is in Contributors'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add explicit error handling (e.g., `set -e`, per-call exit-code checks) and an observability hook (log line / CI failure signal) to the setup-project.sh REST calls that create/populate the Contributors group, so partial-state failures are surfaced immediately rather than silently blocking submits.
findings.finding: setup-project.sh REST calls (create Contributors group, add members) have no described error handling, retry logic, or observability. If the Gerrit REST API fails mid-run (e.g., group created but member-add call fails), the Contributors group will exist but be empty — silently preventing all contributors from landing changes until an admin notices and re-runs the script. No log/metric/alert is specified to surface this partial-state failure.
findings.suggested_fix: In setup-project.sh, wrap each Gerrit REST call in a check: `curl -f ... || { echo 'ERROR: failed to add <member> to Contributors'; exit 1; }`. Run the script with `set -e` globally. In CI, treat a non-zero exit as a blocking failure and emit a visible error message identifying which step failed.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Document (or enforce) host-key pinning for the Gerrit admin SSH connection in setup-project.sh: either pre-populate `~/.ssh/known_hosts` with the Gerrit server's host key and drop `StrictHostKeyChecking=no`, or add a one-time key-fingerprint verification step before the script performs the new Contributors group creation and refs/meta/config push.
findings.finding: setup-project.sh (extended by this plan to add the Contributors group and push refs/meta/config with the new Submit ACL) runs all new admin-privilege SSH operations over a connection built with `StrictHostKeyChecking=no -o UserKnownHostsFile=/dev/null`, disabling Gerrit host-key verification. A MITM between the operator and Gerrit could silently divert or observe the admin private key exchange, group creation commands, member additions, or the refs/meta/config push — making the very ACL intended to gate landing malleable to an attacker on the path. The plan adds new sensitive operations through this unverified channel without acknowledging the risk.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace `StrictHostKeyChecking=no -o UserKnownHostsFile=/dev/null` with `-o StrictHostKeyChecking=yes` and ensure the Gerrit host key is pre-loaded in the operator's `known_hosts` (or a script-local known_hosts file via `-o UserKnownHostsFile=/path/to/pinned_known_hosts`). For CI/unattended use, pin the host key in the CI environment's known_hosts rather than disabling verification entirely.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
